name: Tempe Pawn & Gold
description_short: Tempe Pawn & Gold is a collateral-based lender and precious metals buyer offering pawn loans, title loans, and cash for gold, jewelry, and electronics in the Phoenix metropolitan area.
description_long:
Tempe Pawn & Gold operates as a full-service pawn shop and precious metals buyer serving Tempe, Scottsdale, Mesa, and Chandler, Arizona. The business positions itself as a community-oriented alternative to traditional lending for consumers facing immediate financial needs. According to their website, they emphasize honesty, integrity, and fair market valuations across their lending and buying operations.

The company offers multiple service lines: pawn loans on collateral items (electronics, jewelry, musical instruments, tools), auto title loans and other vehicle-based title loans, direct purchase of gold, diamonds, silver, jewelry, and used electronics. Their pawn loan structure typically runs 90 days with a monthly interest rate; they advertise 0% interest for the first 30 days for new customers who print and bring in their promotional page. Loans can be extended if interest is current. They also operate as a used goods retailer, stocking discounted items in working condition.

Tempe Pawn & Gold distinguishes itself through several claimed differentiators: expert diamond evaluation with precision grading, competitive cash pricing for precious metals, hassle-free processes, instant funding availability, and stated willingness to work with borrowers on payment arrangements. They offer ancillary services including notary public services, police ticket fee payment, and eBay item listings. The business maintains an online presence with customer testimonials and awards recognition.

As a pawn shop, this business operates within regulated lending (both federal and state law govern pawn transactions). However, customers should understand that pawn loans involve forfeiture of collateral if loans aren't repaid by the extended term, and monthly interest rates—while not disclosed on the website—can be substantial. The company's reliance on collateral valuation means loan amounts depend entirely on item assessment. Like all pawn operations, this is a short-term liquidity solution, not a sustainable credit-building tool.
pros: Offers 0% interest for first 30 days on pawn loans for new customers (with promotional materials)
Longer loan term than typical pawn shops (90 days standard, extendable if interest is current)
Multiple asset types accepted: electronics, jewelry, diamonds, watches, musical instruments, tools, laptops, gaming consoles, vehicles (title loans)
Stated commitment to fair pricing and competitive cash offers for gold and precious metals
Same-day or rapid funding available for pawn and title loans
Claims to work with borrowers on payment arrangements and communication
Operates as both lender and retailer, providing access to discounted used goods
cons: Monthly interest rates on pawn loans not disclosed on website; actual APR cost unclear to potential borrowers
Collateral forfeiture after loan term expires with no redemption—higher risk than installment loans
No mention of credit reporting to bureaus, so pawn loans don't build credit history
Loan amounts capped by collateral value assessment, which may be lower than customer expectations
Title loans on vehicles create risk of vehicle loss if loan defaults; less suitable for those relying on transportation
best_for: Consumers needing emergency cash in 24-48 hours without credit checks (e.g., medical bills, urgent repairs)
People with items of value (jewelry, electronics, tools) they can temporarily part with and later reclaim
Vehicle owners with no other lending options seeking fast title-based loans
Those selling gold, diamonds, or precious metals for immediate cash at fair market prices
diagnosis: Tempe Pawn & Gold is best for individuals facing immediate cash shortfalls who have valuable personal property or vehicles to pledge as collateral and can repay within 90 days. The main caveat is that pawn loans carry the risk of permanent item loss if unpaid, don't build credit, and involve undisclosed monthly interest rates—making this a short-term emergency tool, not a long-term financial solution.
services: Pawn loans (90-day terms, monthly interest, extendable)
Cash for gold, silver, and precious metals
Diamond and jewelry buying and lending
Electronics loans and purchases (laptops, phones, gaming consoles, TVs)
Used goods retail (discounted items in working condition)
